name: Via Verde de los Alcores – circular from Alcala de Guadaira to Carmona
name_original: Vía Verde de los Alcores – circular desde Alcalá de Guadaíra a Carmona
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcalá De Guadaíra offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Guadaíra River and its extensive riverbanks, which form a Natural Monument. The terrain includes lush riverine forests, repopulated pine forests, and agricultural fields, with varied relief from gentle undulations to flat plains. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse these natural settings, often encountering historical sites and scenic viewpoints. The region provides a mix of unpaved surfaces, dirt, and gravel roads, alongside some paved sections. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Guadaíra The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde de los Alcores – circular from Alcala de Guadaira to Carmona, a 37.2 miles (59.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an old railway line, offering diverse landscapes and views of historical sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gravel track along the canal – Church of Santiago loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra, a moderate 25.5 miles (41.0 km) path. This route features a gravel track along a canal and passes historical landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Alcalá de Guadaíra – Los Alcores Greenway loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Los Alcores Greenway, often completed in about 4 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá De Guadaíra is defined by the Guadaíra River, historical flour mills, and the Alcores Greenway. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Guadaíra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Guadaíra, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These include 6 easy routes, 45 moderate routes, and 10 more challenging ones.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The terrain is quite varied, featuring lush riverine forests along the Guadaíra River, repopulated pine forests, and agricultural fields. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved surfaces, dirt, and gravel roads, alongside some paved sections, offering a dynamic and visually interesting journey.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Yes, Alcalá De Guadaíra offers several routes suitable for families. The region's generally varied terrain also features stretches of flat relief and dedicated paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31745462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrajas Tower – Vadalejos Bajo Mill loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter, moderate route that can be enjoyed by those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Cycling through Alcalá De Guadaíra allows you to encounter significant historical and cultural landmarks. Many routes offer views of the imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/385597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Guadaíra Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find ancient flour mills along the Guadaíra River, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19360080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle – Los Alcores Greenway loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra&lt;/a&gt; pass by other historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987694298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de los Alcores – circular from Alcala de Guadaira to Carmona&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line and offers diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Alcalá De Guadaíra is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: What natural features can I explore on a touring bike in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The primary natural draw is the Guadaíra River and its riverbanks, which are a Natural Monument. You can cycle through diverse ecosystems, including riverine forests and repopulated pine forests. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38270829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Guadaíra – Los Alcores Greenway loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience these varied landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987694298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de los Alcores – circular from Alcala de Guadaira to Carmona&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent route that originates from an old railway line. It offers a mostly unpaved surface and covers about 25 kilometers, providing a unique cycling experience through diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that feature both natural scenery and historical elements?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra seamlessly blend natural beauty with historical significance. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40394828" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel track along the canal – Church of Santiago loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra&lt;/a&gt; features a scenic gravel track along a canal and passes by historical landmarks, offering a rich experience.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners?
A: Yes, Alcalá De Guadaíra offers 6 easy touring cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically have gentle elevations and are often on well-maintained paths, making them accessible for most cyclists.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Oromana Park, part of the Ribera del Guadaíra Natural Monument, offers spectacular views of the Guadaíra River and the Alcalá de Guadaíra castle from its hills. Many routes are designed to pass through or near these scenic spots, providing excellent photo opportunities.
tips: A route of about 25 kilometers, its origins are found in the now defunct Seville-Alcalá-Carmona railway, also known as "The bakers' train".

The Cordoba Gate is one of Carmona's most emblematic monuments and a living testament to its ancient history. It formed part of the walled enclosure and the route of the Via Augusta, connecting Carmona with Cordoba. Its function was more propagandistic than defensive, conceived as a triumphal arch to symbolize the power and prosperity of the Roman Empire.

Carmona has several viewpoints, but the most well-known is the Mirador de la Asomada del Ribero, located at the entrance to the town, and also the Mirador del Parador, next to the Alcázar of King Pedro. Both offer spectacular views.

The Alcázar of the Seville Gate is one of Carmona's most iconic monuments and an exceptional example of defensive architecture with over 2,500 years of history.

Remains dating back to the 9th century BC have been found, although the current structure began to take shape under the Carthaginians, who built a bastion atop an earlier tower to withstand Roman attacks.
Historic city with some of the most impressive processions during the traditional annual Holy Week celebrations.

The bridge "Jesús Nazareno" (or puente Romano = Roman bridge) is a bridge in Alcalá de Guadaíra (Sevilla, España) over the river Guadaíra. There is no exact historical data, but it is believed to be of Roman origin
